file.content:
Rails.application.routes.draw do
  root "dashboard#index"

  # Mount Action Cable for WebSocket connections
  mount ActionCable.server => "/cable"

  resource :session
  resources :passwords, param: :token

  get  "invites/:token",        to: "registrations#new",    as: :new_registration
  post "invites/:token/accept", to: "registrations#create", as: :accept_registration

  get  "auth/:provider/callback", to: "oauth_callbacks#create"
  post "auth/:provider/callback", to: "oauth_callbacks#create"
  get  "auth/failure",            to: "oauth_callbacks#failure"
  resource :change_password, only: [ :show, :update ]

  resource :settings, only: :show do
    patch :update_profile
    patch :update_password
    patch :toggle_tailscale
    post :generate_token
    delete "revoke_token/:id", action: :revoke_token, as: :revoke_token
  end

  get  "auth/device",              to: "device_auth#show",     as: :auth_device
  post "auth/device/verify",       to: "device_auth#verify",   as: :auth_device_verify
  get  "auth/device/approve/:id",  to: "device_auth#confirm",  as: :auth_device_confirm
  post "auth/device/approve",      to: "device_auth#approve",  as: :auth_device_approve

  resources :sandboxes, only: [ :new, :create, :show, :destroy, :update ] do
    member do
      post :start
      post :stop
      post :retry
      post :archive_restore
      get :logs
      get :stats, controller: "dashboard", action: "stats"
      get :metrics
      get :card, controller: "dashboard", action: "card"
      post :terminal, controller: "terminal", action: "open"
      delete :terminal, controller: "terminal", action: "close"
      post :vnc, controller: "vnc", action: "open"
      delete :vnc, controller: "vnc", action: "close"
      post :snapshot, controller: "snapshots", action: "create_for_sandbox"
    end
    resources :routes, only: [ :create, :destroy ]
  end

  resources :snapshots, only: [ :index, :destroy ], param: :name do
    member do
      post :clone
    end
  end

  get "terminal/auth", to: "terminal#auth"
  get "vnc/auth", to: "vnc#auth"

  resource :tailscale, only: [], controller: "tailscale" do
    get :show
    post :login
    get :login_status
    get :connected
    patch :update_settings
    delete :disable
  end

  namespace :admin do
    get "/", to: "dashboard#index", as: :dashboard
    get "system_status", to: "dashboard#system_status"
    resource :settings, only: [ :edit, :update ]
    resources :users
    resources :invites, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ]
    resources :sandboxes, only: :destroy do
      member do
        post :start
        post :stop
        get :stats
        post :archive_restore
        delete :purge
      end
    end

    resources :docker, only: :index, controller: "docker" do
      get :logs, on: :member
    end

    # Job monitoring dashboard
    mount MissionControl::Jobs::Engine, at: "/jobs"

    # Error tracking dashboard
    mount SolidErrors::Engine, at: "/errors"
  end

  namespace :api do
    get "archived_sandboxes", to: "sandboxes#archived_index"
    resources :sandboxes do
      member do
        post :start
        post :stop
        get :logs
        post :connect
        post :snapshot
        post :restore
        post :archive_restore
        delete :purge
        post :tailscale_connect
        delete :tailscale_disconnect
      end
      resources :routes, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ]
    end
    resources :snapshots, only: [ :index, :create, :show, :destroy ], param: :name
    resources :users
    resource :status, only: :show
    resource :info, only: :show
    resources :tokens, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ]
    namespace :auth do
      post :device_code
      post :device_token
    end
    resource :tailscale, only: [], controller: "tailscale" do
      post :enable
      post :login
      get :login_status
      patch :update_settings
      delete :disable
      get :status
    end
  end

  get "guide", to: "pages#guide"

  get "up" => "rails/health#show", as: :rails_health_check
end

file.content:
class MissionControl::Jobs::ApplicationController < MissionControl::Jobs.base_controller_class.constantize
  ActionController::Base::MODULES.each do |mod|
    include mod unless self < mod
  end

  layout "mission_control/jobs/application"

  # Include helpers if not already included
  helper MissionControl::Jobs::ApplicationHelper unless self < MissionControl::Jobs::ApplicationHelper
  helper Importmap::ImportmapTagsHelper unless self < Importmap::ImportmapTagsHelper

  include MissionControl::Jobs::BasicAuthentication
  include MissionControl::Jobs::ApplicationScoped, MissionControl::Jobs::NotFoundRedirections
  include MissionControl::Jobs::AdapterFeatures
  include MissionControl::Jobs::JobFilters

  around_action :set_current_locale

  private
    def default_url_options
      { server_id: MissionControl::Jobs::Current.server }
    end

    def set_current_locale(&block)
      @previous_config = I18n.config
      I18n.config = MissionControl::Jobs::I18nConfig.new
      I18n.with_locale(:en, &block)
    ensure
      I18n.config = @previous_config
      @previous_config = nil
    end
end

content.text: Both engines override the layout with their own (`solid_errors/application` and `mission_control/jobs/application`). That's why they don't use the admin layout. To make them use the admin layout, I need to override their layouts. The cleanest way is to create layout files in the app that override the engine ones.

  /*
  1. Reset the default placeholder opacity in Firefox. (https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss/issues/3300)
  2. Set the default placeholder color to the user's configured gray 400 color.
  */

  input::placeholder,
  textarea::placeholder {
    opacity: 1;
    /* 1 */
    color: #9ca3af;
    /* 2 */
  }

  /*
  Set the default cursor for buttons.
  */

  button,
  [role="button"] {
    cursor: pointer;
  }

  /*
  Make sure disabled buttons don't get the pointer cursor.
  */

  :disabled {
    cursor: default;
  }

  /*
  1. Make replaced elements `display: block` by default. (https://github.com/mozdevs/cssremedy/issues/14)
  2. Add `vertical-align: middle` to align replaced elements more sensibly by default. (https://github.com/jensimmons/cssremedy/issues/14#issuecomment-634934210)
    This can trigger a poorly considered lint error in some tools but is included by design.
  */

  img,
  svg,
  video,
  canvas,
  audio,
  iframe,
  embed,
  object {
    display: block;
    /* 1 */
    vertical-align: middle;
    /* 2 */
  }

  /*
  Constrain images and videos to the parent width and preserve their intrinsic aspect ratio. (https://github.com/mozdevs/cssremedy/issues/14)
  */

  img,
  video {
    max-width: 100%;
    height: auto;
  }

  /* Make elements with the HTML hidden attribute stay hidden by default */

  [hidden] {
    display: none;
  }

content.text:
SolidErrors bundles its own Tailwind CSS inline. MissionControl uses Bulma. Both will conflict with our Tailwind v4. The cleanest approach: override each layout to just use our admin layout, wrapping engine content in a scoped `<div>` and including the engine's styles.

Let me create the override layouts:

input.content:
<% content_for(:title, "Errors - Admin - Sandcastle") %> <% content_for(:head) do %> <%= render "layouts/solid_errors/style" %> <% end %>
<%= content_for?(:content) ? yield(:content) : yield %>

content.text:
Wait — these engine layouts specify `layout "solid_errors/application"` etc., but now these are just partials within the admin layout. The problem is: since both engines inherit from `Admin::BaseController` which has `layout "admin"`, but then the engine controllers override with their own layout name. My override files replace the engine's layout, but they need to render *inside* the admin layout.

The trick is: these override layout files are what gets rendered as the layout. Since the engine controller says `layout "solid_errors/application"`, Rails will use my override file. But this file needs to be a full layout OR delegate to the admin layout.

Let me use `render layout:` to nest inside admin:

input.content:
<% content_for(:title, "Errors - Sandcastle") %> <% content_for(:head) do %> <%= render "layouts/solid_errors/style" %> <% end %> <%= render layout: "layouts/admin" do %>
<%= content_for?(:content) ? yield(:content) : yield %>
<% end %>

content.text:
Actually, wait. `render layout:` inside a layout creates a nested rendering which won't work correctly here. The right approach is simpler: since both engines already inherit from `Admin::BaseController` which sets `layout "admin"`, I just need the engine controllers to NOT override the layout. But I can't change the gem code.

A better approach: make the engine layout files just yield content (no `<html>` wrapper), and Rails will insert that into the admin layout since `Admin::BaseController` already sets `layout "admin"`.

Actually, let me re-think. When Rails sees `layout "solid_errors/application"`, it uses that layout file as the full HTML wrapper. The `layout "admin"` from the base controller is overridden by the engine's layout. My override replaces the engine layout file but it still needs to be a complete document or delegate.

The cleanest solution: just make the override layout files delegate to admin.

input.content:
<%= render template: "layouts/admin" do %>
<%= render "layouts/solid_errors/style" %> <%= content_for?(:content) ? yield(:content) : yield %>
<% end %>

file.content: <% admin_tabs = [ { label: "Overview", path: main_app.admin_dashboard_path, icon: "overview", active: controller_name == "dashboard" }, { label: "Users", path: main_app.admin_users_path, icon: "users", active: controller_name == "users" }, { label: "Invites", path: main_app.admin_invites_path, icon: "invites", active: controller_name == "invites" }, { label: "Settings", path: main_app.edit_admin_settings_path, icon: "settings", active: controller_name == "settings" }, { label: "Docker", path: main_app.admin_docker_index_path, icon: "docker", active: controller_name == "docker" }, { label: "Jobs", path: "/admin/jobs", icon: "jobs", active: request.path.start_with?("/admin/jobs") }, { label: "Errors", path: "/admin/errors", icon: "errors", active: request.path.start_with?("/admin/errors"), badge: (SolidErrors::Error.unresolved.count rescue 0) }, ] %> <% admin_tabs.each do |tab| %> <%= link_to tab[:path], class: "group inline-flex items-center gap-2 px-4 py-3 text-sm font-medium border-b-2 transition-colors #{ tab[:active] ? 'border-blue-600 text-blue-600' : 'border-transparent text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-700 hover:border-gray-300' }" do %> <% case tab[:icon] %> <% when "overview" %> <% when "users" %> <% when "invites" %> <% when "settings" %> <% when "docker" %> <% when "jobs" %> <% when "errors" %> <% end %> <%= tab[:label] %> <% if tab[:badge].to_i > 0 %> <%= tab[:badge] %> <% end %> <% end %> <% end %>

content.text: OK, let me step back and use the approach that's known to work in Rails: create override layout files that use `parent_layout`. This is Rails' built-in nested layout pattern.

The nested layout pattern in Rails: in the child layout, wrap the yield in `content_for`, then render the parent. Or simpler: the child layout just yields, and the controller uses the parent layout. But we can't change the engine controller's `layout` line.

The actual correct Rails pattern for engine layout override with nesting:

input.content:
<%= csrf_meta_tags %> <%= csp_meta_tag %> <%= stylesheet_link_tag :app, "data-turbo-track": "reload" %> <%= javascript_importmap_tags %> <% if authenticated? %> <%= render "shared/navbar" %> <% end %>
<%= render "admin/subnav" %>
<%= content_for?(:content) ? yield(:content) : yield %>
<%= Sandcastle.version %>

content.text: Wait, I'm duplicating the entire admin layout now. That's not DRY. Let me actually just use the `render layout:` approach which IS the correct Rails nested layout mechanism:

input.content:
<%= render layout: "layouts/admin" do %>
  <%= content_for?(:content) ? yield(:content) : yield %>
<% end %>

content.text:
Wait, `render layout:` is for rendering a template with a layout wrapping it — it's meant for rendering partials with a layout, not for nesting full page layouts. The actual method for nesting layouts is different.

Let me just test if this approach works in the dev environment:

file.content:
rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists /var/cache/apt/archives

# Create user early
RUN groupadd --system --gid 220568 sandcastle && \
    useradd sandcastle --uid 220568 --gid 220568 --create-home --shell /bin/bash && \
    groupadd --system docker && \
    usermod -aG docker sandcastle

# Switch to development bundle config
ENV RAILS_ENV="development" \
    BUNDLE_PATH="/usr/local/bundle" \
    BUNDLE_WITHOUT=""

# Install all gems (including development/test)
COPY Gemfile Gemfile.lock ./
# Install libyaml-dev for psych gem
RUN apt-get update -qq && \
    apt-get install --no-install-recommends -y libyaml-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists /var/cache/apt/archives
RUN bundle install

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /rails

# Run as sandcastle user
USER 220568:220568

# Entrypoint for db readiness
ENTRYPOINT ["/rails/bin/docker-entrypoint"]

content.text: The dev target copies Gemfile/Gemfile.lock and runs `bundle install`. But I ran `bundle update` locally, updating Gemfile.lock. The Docker build uses the updated lock file. But it still says it can't find gems. Maybe Docker is caching the layer.

diff --git a/app/services/sandbox_manager.rb b/app/services/sandbox_manager.rb
index 088e745..d1f39e6 100644
--- a/app/services/sandbox_manager.rb
+++ b/app/services/sandbox_manager.rb
@@ -696,18 +696,34 @@ class SandboxManager
   end
 
   def docker_run_fix(host_path, *cmd)
-    image = "busybox:latest"
-    Docker::Image.get(image) rescue Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => image)
+    image = fix_image
     c = Docker::Container.create(
       "Image" => image, "Cmd" => cmd,
       "HostConfig" => { "Binds" => [ "#{host_path}:/mnt" ] }
     )
     c.start
-    c.wait(10)
+    result = c.wait(30)
+    exit_code = result&.dig("StatusCode") || -1
+    unless exit_code == 0
+      raise Error, "docker_run_fix failed (exit #{exit_code}) for #{host_path}: #{cmd.join(' ')}"
+    end
   ensure
     c&.delete(force: true) rescue nil
   end
 
+  # Pick an image guaranteed to be on this Docker daemon.
+  # Prefer busybox (tiny), fall back to alpine, then any local image.
+  def fix_image
+    %w[busybox:latest alpine:latest].each do |img|
+      return img if Docker::Image.get(img) rescue nil
+    end
+    # Last resort: use any image already present
+    all = Docker::Image.all
+    raise Error, "No local images available for docker_run_fix" if all.empty?
+    tags = all.first.info["RepoTags"]
+    tags&.first || all.first.id
+  end
+
   def volume_binds(user, sandbox)
     binds = []
     if sandbox.mount_home

diff --git a/app/services/sandbox_manager.rb b/app/services/sandbox_manager.rb
index 088e745..d1f39e6 100644
--- a/app/services/sandbox_manager.rb
+++ b/app/services/sandbox_manager.rb
@@ -696,18 +696,34 @@ class SandboxManager
   end
 
   def docker_run_fix(host_path, *cmd)
-    image = "busybox:latest"
-    Docker::Image.get(image) rescue Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => image)
+    image = fix_image
     c = Docker::Container.create(
       "Image" => image, "Cmd" => cmd,
       "HostConfig" => { "Binds" => [ "#{host_path}:/mnt" ] }
     )
     c.start
-    c.wait(10)
+    result = c.wait(30)
+    exit_code = result&.dig("StatusCode") || -1
+    unless exit_code == 0
+      raise Error, "docker_run_fix failed (exit #{exit_code}) for #{host_path}: #{cmd.join(' ')}"
+    end
   ensure
     c&.delete(force: true) rescue nil
   end
 
+  # Pick an image guaranteed to be on this Docker daemon.
+  # Prefer busybox (tiny), fall back to alpine, then any local image.
+  def fix_image
+    %w[busybox:latest alpine:latest].each do |img|
+      return img if Docker::Image.get(img) rescue nil
+    end
+    # Last resort: use any image already present
+    all = Docker::Image.all
+    raise Error, "No local images available for docker_run_fix" if all.empty?
+    tags = all.first.info["RepoTags"]
+    tags&.first || all.first.id
+  end
+
   def volume_binds(user, sandbox)
     binds = []
     if sandbox.mount_home

content.text:
Hmm, these use `render layout:` which is actually a partial rendering approach, not a layout nesting. For SolidErrors I already have the initializer approach (`SolidErrors::ApplicationController.layout "admin"`). The layout override file AND the initializer would conflict. Let me pick ONE approach. 

The initializer approach is cleaner for SolidErrors since it uses standard Tailwind classes our CSS already covers. For MissionControl, I need the layout override to inject Bulma CSS. Let me use:
- SolidErrors: initializer only (no layout override file)  
- MissionControl: layout override file only (no initializer)

input.description: Remove solid_errors layout override (using initializer instead)

content.text:
Now for MissionControl, `render layout: "layouts/admin"` won't work as I intended — `render layout:` renders a partial using another partial as a wrapper, not a full page layout nesting. Let me instead use the initializer approach for MissionControl too, and inject Bulma CSS differently.

Actually, the simplest solution: just use the initializer to set `layout "admin"` for both engines. MissionControl views will look unstyled without Bulma, but let's see if it's acceptable — the navigation tabs etc. will just use default browser styles. Or better: we can include Bulma in the admin layout conditionally.

content.text:
Done. Here's a summary of everything:

**Bug fix - `docker_run_fix`:**
- `fix_image` now tries busybox, then alpine, then falls back to any locally available image (instead of hardcoding `busybox:latest` which may not exist on the Dockyard daemon)
- Container exit code is now checked — raises `SandboxManager::Error` on non-zero exit
- Wait timeout increased from 10s to 30s

**Tests:**
- Added `test/services/ensure_dir_test.rb` with 6 test cases covering ensure_dir, docker_run_fix, and fix_image
- Docker mock now includes `wait` method and a default busybox image

**Admin layout for engines:**
- SolidErrors and MissionControl::Jobs now use the admin layout (with subnav) via `after_initialize` overrides
- Bulma CSS/JS injected in admin layout for `/admin/jobs` paths

**Gems:** All updated to latest versions.

**Released as v0.8.45.**

file.content:
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.6
    container_name: sandcastle-traefik
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
      - "8443:443"
      - "3000-3099:3000-3099"
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/etc/traefik
      - ./.cert:/certs:ro
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web
    depends_on:
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  init-traefik:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - sh
      - -c
      - |
        mkdir -p /data/dynamic
        if [ ! -f /data/traefik.yml ]; then
          cat > /data/traefik.yml << 'EOF'
        entryPoints:
          web:
            address: ":80"
          websecure:
            address: ":443"
        providers:
          file:
            directory: /etc/traefik/dynamic
            watch: true
        log:
          level: INFO
        EOF
          cat > /data/dynamic/rails.yml << 'EOFCONFIG'
        http:
          routers:
            rails-http:
              rule: "Host(`${DEV_HOST}`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - web
            rails-https:
              rule: "Host(`${DEV_HOST}`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - websecure
              tls: {}
          services:
            rails:
              loadBalancer:
                servers:
                  - url: "http://sandcastle-web:80"
        EOFCONFIG
        fi
        chown -R 220568:220568 /data

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "7633:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_development
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_development"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  init-data:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - sandcastle-data:/data
    command: sh -c "mkdir -p /data/users /data/sandboxes && chown -R 220568:220568 /data"

  web:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "dev"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: ""
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: ""
        BUILD_DATE: ""
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live reloading
      - .:/rails
      # Persist gems in volume (faster than bind mount)
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
      # Persist node modules
      - node-modules:/rails/node_modules
      # Share Docker socket
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Data directory
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      # Traefik config
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      PORT: 80
      BINDING: 0.0.0.0
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: ${DEV_HOST}
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR: /certs
      SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://${DEV_HOST}:8443
      SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL: https://${DEV_HOST}:8443
      SANDCASTLE_TRAEFIK_CONFIG: /data/traefik/traefik.yml
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN: "3000"
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX: "3099"
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: ./bin/dev
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  worker:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "dev"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: ""
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: ""
        BUILD_DATE: ""
    container_name: sandcastle-dev-worker
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live reloading
      - .:/rails
      # Persist gems in volume (faster than bind mount)
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
      # Share Docker socket
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Data directory
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      # Traefik config
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: ${DEV_HOST}
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR: /certs
      SANDCASTLE_TRAEFIK_CONFIG: /data/traefik/traefik.yml
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN: "3000"
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX: "3099"
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle

content.text: Traefik mounts `.cert` to `/certs` and uses `tls: {}` on the HTTPS router. But there's no TLS config pointing to the cert files. Traefik needs either a `tls` section in its dynamic config or a `certificates` store. Let me add that to the init-traefik config:

services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.6
    container_name: sandcastle-traefik
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
      - "8443:443"
      - "3000-3099:3000-3099"
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/etc/traefik
      - ./.cert:/certs:ro
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web
    depends_on:
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  init-traefik:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - sh
      - -c
      - |
        mkdir -p /data/dynamic
        if [ ! -f /data/traefik.yml ]; then
          cat > /data/traefik.yml << 'EOF'
        entryPoints:
          web:
            address: ":80"
          websecure:
            address: ":443"
        providers:
          file:
            directory: /etc/traefik/dynamic
            watch: true
        log:
          level: INFO
        EOF
          cat > /data/dynamic/rails.yml << 'EOFCONFIG'
        http:
          routers:
            rails-http:
              rule: "Host(`${DEV_HOST}`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - web
            rails-https:
              rule: "Host(`${DEV_HOST}`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - websecure
              tls: {}
          services:
            rails:
              loadBalancer:
                servers:
                  - url: "http://sandcastle-web:80"
        EOFCONFIG
        fi
        chown -R 220568:220568 /data

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "7633:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_development
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_development"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  init-data:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - sandcastle-data:/data
    command: sh -c "mkdir -p /data/users /data/sandboxes && chown -R 220568:220568 /data"

  web:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "dev"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: ""
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: ""
        BUILD_DATE: ""
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live reloading
      - .:/rails
      # Persist gems in volume (faster than bind mount)
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
      # Persist node modules
      - node-modules:/rails/node_modules
      # Share Docker socket
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Data directory
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      # Traefik config
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      PORT: 80
      BINDING: 0.0.0.0
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: ${DEV_HOST}
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR: /certs
      SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://${DEV_HOST}:8443
      SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL: https://${DEV_HOST}:8443
      SANDCASTLE_TRAEFIK_CONFIG: /data/traefik/traefik.yml
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN: "3000"
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX: "3099"
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: ./bin/dev
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  worker:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "dev"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: ""
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: ""
        BUILD_DATE: ""
    container_name: sandcastle-dev-worker
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live reloading
      - .:/rails
      # Persist gems in volume (faster than bind mount)
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
      # Share Docker socket
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Data directory
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      # Traefik config
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: ${DEV_HOST}
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR: /certs
      SANDCASTLE_TRAEFIK_CONFIG: /data/traefik/traefik.yml
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN: "3000"
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX: "3099"
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: ["bundle", "exec", "rake", "solid_queue:start"]
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  migrate:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
    volumes:
      - .:/rails
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
    command: ["./bin/rails", "db:prepare"]
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER: thies
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL: thieso@gmail.com
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD: tubu
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY: "ssh-ed25519 REDACTED thieso@gmail.com"
      SANDCASTLE_SKIP_PASSWORD_CHANGE: "true"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

networks:
  sandcastle-web:
    external: true

volumes:
  bundle-data:
  node-modules:
  pgdata:
  sandcastle-data:
  traefik-data:

          certificates:
            - certFile: /certs/cert.pem
              keyFile: /certs/key.pem
        EOFTLS
        fi
        chown -R 220568:220568 /data

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "7633:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_development
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_development"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  init-data:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - sandcastle-data:/data
    command: sh -c "mkdir -p /data/users /data/sandboxes && chown -R 220568:220568 /data"

  web:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "dev"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: ""
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: ""
        BUILD_DATE: ""
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live reloading
      - .:/rails
      # Persist gems in volume (faster than bind mount)
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
      # Persist node modules
      - node-modules:/rails/node_modules
      # Share Docker socket
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Data directory
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      # Traefik config
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      PORT: 80
      BINDING: 0.0.0.0
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: ${DEV_HOST}
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR: /certs
      SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://${DEV_HOST}:8443
      SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL: https://${DEV_HOST}:8443
      SANDCASTLE_TRAEFIK_CONFIG: /data/traefik/traefik.yml
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN: "3000"
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX: "3099"
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: ./bin/dev
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  worker:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "dev"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: ""
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: ""
        BUILD_DATE: ""
    container_name: sandcastle-dev-worker
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live reloading
      - .:/rails
      # Persist gems in volume (faster than bind mount)
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
      # Share Docker socket
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Data directory
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      # Traefik config
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: ${DEV_HOST}
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR: /certs
      SANDCASTLE_TRAEFIK_CONFIG: /data/traefik/traefik.yml
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN: "3000"
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX: "3099"
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: ["bundle", "exec", "rake", "solid_queue:start"]
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  migrate:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
    volumes:
      - .:/rails
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
    command: ["./bin/rails", "db:prepare"]
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER: thies
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL: thieso@gmail.com
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD: tubu
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY: "ssh-ed25519 REDACTED thieso@gmail.com"
      SANDCASTLE_SKIP_PASSWORD_CHANGE: "true"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

networks:
  sandcastle-web:
    external: true

volumes:
  bundle-data:
  node-modules:
  pgdata:
  sandcastle-data:
  traefik-data:
